monthly_summary: The COVID-19 pandemic led to significant disruptions in 2020. The English Premier League was suspended due to the coronavirus outbreak. The pandemic also affected major sporting events, with UEFA cancelling hotel bookings for the Euro 2020 host city of Copenhagen. Additionally, the US military was impacted, with 174 service members testing positive for the virus and a US Army Europe general working remotely.

monthly_summary: In February 2020, the COVID-19 pandemic continued to spread globally. A drone in China was deployed to monitor social distancing, but it inadvertently caught villagers playing mahjong, highlighting the challenges in enforcing lockdowns. In the US, a trial was planned for the antiviral drug remdesivir, developed by Gilead, as a potential treatment for COVID-19. Meanwhile, pharmaceutical companies like GSK and Clover were evaluating a COVID-19 vaccine candidate, offering hope for a future solution to the pandemic.

monthly_summary: In October 2016, the US presidential election was heating up. Michelle Obama delivered a speech in North Carolina, rallying support for Hillary Clinton. Meanwhile, Donald Trump's campaign was experiencing a meltdown, prompting him to focus on the upcoming debates to regain momentum. Pence criticized the timing of unsubstantiated claims against Trump, highlighting the party's efforts to address the controversy and maintain a united front ahead of the election.

monthly_summary: In November 2016, the Republican Party maintained control of the Indiana governorship. Meanwhile, former Obama advisor David Axelrod suggested that President Obama might meet with President-elect Trump as early as Thursday. Despite efforts to suppress Democratic votes, voter-intimidation cases in three US states were unsuccessful. The election also highlighted the low voter turnout, with nearly half of Americans not casting a ballot, including a notable example of people not voting for the presidential election.

monthly_summary: In February 2020, the U.S. Chamber of Commerce expressed its intention to support Democratic allies in the 2020 elections. Meanwhile, Joe Biden urged Bernie Sanders to take responsibility for his supporters' threats against a culinary union. The Iowa caucus results were disputed, with Bernie Sanders declaring victory despite incomplete results. Donald Trump rallied supporters in Nevada, wrapping up a four-day trip to the West, as the Democratic primary heated up with discussions surrounding Bernie Sanders' democratic socialism ideology.

monthly_summary: In February 2016, the US presidential election was gaining momentum. Marco Rubio received a boost from Republican endorsements and a polling bump after Iowa, while Ted Cruz faced criticism for a misstep. Rubio also gained attention for mocking Donald Trump's alleged "wet" pants. Meanwhile, Cruz floated the idea of Phil Robertson, a star from the reality TV show "Duck Dynasty," as a potential UN ambassador, highlighting the unconventional nature of the Republican primary.

monthly_summary: In February 2016, the Zika virus was reported to have infected 3,177 pregnant women in Colombia, with no reported birth defects yet. The first U.S. Zika virus transmission was also reported, attributed to sex. This news came amidst the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, which had not yet been identified. The global health community was already focused on the Zika virus, a mosquito-borne disease linked to birth defects, and its spread through human-to-human contact.

Henderson, Doctor Who Helped End Smallpox Scourge, Dies at 87', 'UPDATE 1-GM mosquito trial will not significantly impact environment-FDA', 'Turns Out Mosquitoes Are Hardly Bothering Tourists At The Olympics', 'Global polio eradication hopes hang in the balance after Nigeria setback', 'U.S. judge upholds U.N. immunity in Haiti cholera case', 'Few fear Zika following first mosquito-borne cases in US', 'Zika virus: Miami Beach mayor calls for federal bill']
monthly_summary: In August 2016, the world grappled with various health crises. D.A. Henderson, a renowned doctor who helped eradicate smallpox, passed away at 87. Meanwhile, a trial using genetically modified mosquitoes to combat Zika and other diseases showed minimal environmental impact. The global fight against polio was dealt a setback in Nigeria, casting uncertainty over the goal of eradication.
